regimeSub: Higher-for-longer regime: Warsh Fed holds at 3.63% with hike bias as CPI runs 4.2%, even as the Iran war fades and oil collapses.
themes: Iran war de-escalation + Hormuz tankers flowing -> Brent back below $74, but disinflation help arrives too late for the Fed.
CPI at 3-year high of 4.2% -> Warsh strips cutting bias, markets now price a September HIKE (hysteresis risk).
Negative real policy rate (3.63% vs 4.2% CPI) -> gold rips to $4,012 as a hedge against credibility loss.
Hawkish repricing + Apple/Big Tech rollover -> SPX heads for a losing week despite Micron-led memory pop.

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% minus CPI 4.2% = negative real rate; Fed is still behind the curve despite the hawkish hold.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +30bp and modestly steeper; bear-steepening risk as hike bias lifts the front and term premium builds.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Still contained with VIX sub-19; no credit stress yet, but a September hike scenario is the squeeze risk.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 18.89 — elevated but calm; Hormuz attack headlines and tech wobble keep a bid under vol, not panic.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $74 and falling to pre-Iran-war levels as Hormuz reopens and UAE supply surges; geopolitical premium bleeding out.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4,012 — record territory; negative real rates and Fed-credibility doubts trump the oil/geopolitics de-escalation.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1,549 — won soft despite weak DXY (101.5); KRW pressured by tech-cycle risk and broad EM-Asia caution.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Two-sided: Micron/memory ripping on AI demand but Apple/Big Tech dragging; leadership narrow, not broad.

headline: The Iran war and its oil spike are over, but inflation isn't — at 4.2% CPI the Warsh Fed holds higher-for-longer with a hike bias, sending gold to records, tech wobbling, and oil back to pre-war lows.
